# Aspect Ratio

Keeps media and other content at a predictable width-to-height ratio.

## Installation

Add Aspect Ratio from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/aspect-ratio
```

## Usage

Give the component a numeric `ratio`, such as `16 / 9`, and size its width from the surrounding layout. The height follows automatically. Make the child fill the frame and choose `object-cover` or `object-contain` according to whether cropping is acceptable.

```vue
<template>
  <div class="w-full max-w-2xl">
    <AspectRatio :ratio="16 / 9" class="overflow-hidden rounded-md bg-muted">
      <img
        src="https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1588345921523-c2dcdb7f1dcd?w=1200&dpr=2&q=80"
        alt="Abstract white architectural surface"
        class="size-full object-cover"
      >
    </AspectRatio>
  </div>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { AspectRatio } from '@/Components/AspectRatio'
</script>
```

## Responsive media

Aspect Ratio is width-driven, so place width and maximum-width classes on a parent or on the component. It does not impose a fixed pixel size. This makes one composition work across breakpoints while preventing layout shift as images or embeds load.

Use the default ratio of `1` for square content. Match the ratio to the intended presentation rather than the source asset when a consistent gallery or card grid matters more than showing every pixel.

## Semantics and accessibility

The component controls geometry, not meaning. Keep useful image `alt` text, video captions, iframe titles, and interactive controls on the slotted content. Do not use Aspect Ratio to replace intrinsic media dimensions when the natural proportions must remain visible.

## Recommendations

- Use it for thumbnails, video frames, maps, and predictable media slots.
- Avoid it for prose or content whose height should grow naturally.
- Use CSS `aspect-ratio` directly when no reusable Vue composition is needed.
- The root forwards Reka primitive props, including `as` and `as-child`; read the [composition fundamentals](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/fundamentals/composition) before changing the rendered element.

Hosted documentation: [Aspect Ratio](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/aspect-ratio)
